content.text: Perfect! Now let me create a comprehensive summary with all the findings. Let me also check the actual content of one Home.tsx file to provide context.

Perfect! I now have all the information needed to provide a comprehensive report. Let me compile the findings:

## Summary Report: Home.tsx File Discovery

I successfully found the Home.tsx file in multiple projects on your system. Here are the exact paths and project details:

### Home.tsx Files Found

**3 Home.tsx files located:**

1. **C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\pages\Home.tsx** (37.7 KB)
   - Project: citedby-ai (Main production project)
   - Modified: 2026-02-22 01:34
   - Component: React functional component with hooks for free audit functionality

2. **C:\Users\User\CitedByAstroLab\pages\Home.tsx** (36.9 KB)
   - Project: CitedByAstroLab (Mirror/staging version)
   - Modified: 2026-02-17 20:49
   - Component: Identical structure to citedby-ai version

3. **C:\Users\User\SaaSV2\pages\Home.tsx** (15.6 KB)
   - Project: SaaSV2 (Simplified SaaS version)
   - Modified: 2026-02-16 05:09
   - Component: Streamlined React component with EEAT score simulation

### Project Structure Overview

All three projects follow a similar architecture:

```
Project Root/
├── pages/                 # Page components (36 in citedby-ai/CitedByAstroLab, 11 in SaaSV2)
│   ├── Home.tsx          # Landing page/dashboard home
│   ├── Login.tsx
│   ├── Analyze.tsx
│   └── [31+ other pages]
├── components/           # Reusable UI components (15+ components)
├── services/            # Business logic & API services (20+ services)
├── types/               # TypeScript type definitions
├── i18n/                # Internationalization (FR/EN/ES)
├── lib/                 # Utility functions & libraries
├── App.tsx              # Main routing component (44KB)
├── index.tsx            # React entry point
├── package.json         # Dependencies
└── tsconfig.json        # TypeScript configuration
```

### Home.tsx Component Details

**citedby-ai & CitedByAstroLab versions** (virtually identical):
- React FC with TypeScript
- Props: `onNavigate`, `user`, `onDirectRecharge`
- State: Brand name, site URL, audit results, loading states
- Features: Free audit form, E-E-A-T pillar scoring (Experience, Expertise, Authority, Trust)
- Uses: Lucide React icons, ScoreRing component, answer-first-audit service
- UI: Dark theme with Tailwind CSS

**SaaSV2 version** (simplified):
- More basic implementation
- Deterministic EEAT score simulation based on input hash
- Fewer features (no real audit, just demo scoring)
- Same component prop structure

### Other Key Files in Projects

**Package Management Files:**
- C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\package.json
- C:\Users\User\CitedByAstroLab\package.json
- C:\Users\User\SaaSV2\package.json

**Configuration Files:**
- tsconfig.json (TypeScript config)
- tailwind.config.ts (Tailwind CSS with dark theme)
- next.config.mjs (Next.js backend config)
- .env.local, .env.example (Environment variables)

### Technology Stack (All Projects)

- **Frontend:** React 18, Vite (dev/build)
- **Styling:** Tailwind CSS (dark theme, purple/violet accents)
- **Icons:** Lucide React
- **AI:** Google Gemini API (@google/genai)
- **Auth:** Supabase
- **Payment:** Stripe
- **i18n:** Custom React Context system
- **Type Safety:** TypeScript, Zod validation
- **Charts:** Recharts

All three projects are JavaScript/TypeScript SPA applications with Vite build tools and Next.js backends, following the standards defined in your CLAUDE.md project instructions.
